L1_dust_replication.key_result: L1 dust-Gamma_t confirmed across the three primary JWST photometric surveys (Fisher z = 24sigma); adding the supplementary NIRSpec Ha/Hb branch leaves the 4-dataset Fisher combination at 25sigma, while the Balmer partial-rho branch itself is null after M*, z control (rho=0.123, p=0.000).

L2_core_screening.gradient_sign_test_assessment: The resolved sign test is directionally consistent with TEP after converting the problem into a high-vs-low screening comparison, but the effect remains short of a decisive inversion detection.

L4_dynamical_mass.sigma_pilot_assessment: Within the fitted DJA pilot subset, the Balmer dust proxy tracks the fitted emission-line width at least as strongly as photometric stellar mass after mass-plus-redshift control, which is the intended direction for the sigma-based decoupling audit.

L5_kinematic_decisive.direct_kinematic_package_caveat: Only the SUSPENSE branch is a direct age-based predictor-comparison test. The object-level M*/Mdyn branch strengthens the broader direct kinematic package, but it remains auxiliary because it uses a different observable. The DJA sigma-Balmer pilot is surfaced as contextual pilot evidence rather than counted supportive evidence because the current supportive subset is very small and relies entirely on fallback spectral-resolution handling. The same-regime literature sample is surfaced as contextual direct-regime coverage and is not counted as a TEP sign-test branch. The sigma-based kinematic expansion branch is surfaced as contextual because its assessment did not meet the supportive threshold.

limitations: The environment-density branch remains inconclusive and sign-mixed.
The strongest supportive tests are residualization and shuffled-null checks rather than direct mass-independent kinematics.
A definitive closure still requires an object-level dynamical calibration.
mass_proxy_breaker.overall_conclusion: 2/3 mass-proxy breaking tests passed. The current test set substantially narrows the mass-proxy degeneracy, especially in the z>8 residual and shuffled-null branches, but it does not close the case without direct mass-independent kinematic calibration.

mass_independent_proxy_suite.conclusion: At z>8, 4/5 indirect proxies associate with dust after mass+z control, but only 3/5 also track Γt directly and 2/5 satisfy both criteria with a consistent depth ordering. The proxy suite therefore narrows — but does not close — the mass-proxy degeneracy.
mass_independent_proxy_suite.honest_caveat: All five proxies are INDIRECT measures of potential depth. They are partially correlated with mass through astrophysical channels unrelated to TEP. A definitive test requires a mass-independent dynamical measurement of potential depth, such as NIRSpec IFU velocity dispersions for z>8 galaxies. This test is proposed as a Cycle 4 JWST program.

kappa_gal_concordance.verdict: PASS: 1 informative observables are mutually concordant and consistent with the external Cepheid calibration (0.3σ tension)

cross_survey_generalization.interpretation: Polynomial generalizes comparably or better than t_eff.

environmental_screening.overall_conclusion: 2/4 redshift bins show the predicted environmental screening pattern (rho_field > rho_dense). Primary test (z > 8): delta_rho = 0.111, Z = 1.16, p = 0.2451.

nested_bayesian_model_comparison.joint_key_finding: Multi-observable joint nested Bayesian model comparison yields mixed results: 0/4 favour TEP while 4/4 favour alternatives. Mean ln(BF) across 4 alternatives = -79.7 (log10 = -34.6).  TEP achieves this with the fewest parameters, leveraging a single theory-fixed predictor (Gamma_t) across all observables.
nested_bayesian_model_comparison.residual_key_finding: Residual-space nested Bayesian comparison remains mixed after mass+z control because Constrained_AGN still competes with TEP. After removing linear mass+z trends from both observables and competing predictors, mean ln(BF) across 2 alternatives = -40.0 (log10 = -17.4).

stellar_mass_function_resolution.conclusion: TEP mass correction resolves 19% of the SMF excess on average. At z=9, correction of 0.05 dex resolves 7% of the 1.1 dex excess. Labbe+2023 anomalous galaxies: 8/9 resolved (89%).

claim_hierarchy.headline_primary_result: L1 dust-Gamma_t replication across three independent JWST surveys remains the primary large-sample JWST result
claim_hierarchy.headline_direct_test: L5 JWST-SUSPENSE direct kinematic comparison materially narrows the photometric mass-circularity objection

verdict.headline_primary_result: L1 dust-Gamma_t replication across three independent JWST surveys
verdict.headline_direct_test: L5 JWST-SUSPENSE direct kinematic comparison materially narrows the photometric mass-circularity objection
